baseline.summary: With a strong foundation in computer science and a passion for building systems where correctness is verifiable, I am excited to join Lucenta AI as an AI Tools Engineering Intern. My projects, such as Meridian (a multi-agent research system) and Anchor (an n8n-orchestrated job-pipeline), demonstrate my proficiency in Python and experience with LLM-based tools like Ollama and Anthropic API, aligning well with the role requirements.

lines.text: At KuKClean (Bangalore, Jun–Jul 2024), set up 20+ product listings end-to-end on the e-commerce storefront and built Figma wireframes for new product pages, showcasing my hands-on experience with Python and LLM-based tools.

Designed and shipped Meridian, a multi-agent orchestration system using Python, FastAPI, and Ollama (qwen2.5:7b), which demonstrates my proficiency in building internal prototypes that use LLMs to automate research tasks.

Developed Meridian, a 4-agent orchestration system with a financial agent that produced cited due-diligence memos on NSE/BSE companies in ~75 seconds, highlighting my experience with prompt engineering for structured outputs.

Built Anchor, an n8n-orchestrated job-pipeline system that uses Playwright to scrape job descriptions and generate factually-grounded application packets, demonstrating my hands-on experience with Python and LLM-based tools.
Proficient in Python, having built complex systems like Meridian and Anchor.
Experience with LLMs through projects such as Meridian (using Ollama) and Anchor (using Anthropic API).
Familiarity with prompt engineering for structured outputs, critical for building internal prototypes that use LLMs.

Pursuing a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science at RV College of Engineering (2024–2028), with a strong academic background and ongoing pursuit of machine learning specializations from Stanford Online.

cover_letter_paragraphs: I am excited to apply for the AI Tools Engineering Intern position at Lucenta AI. My background in building systems that prioritize correctness aligns well with your team's research-adjacent and fast-iteration culture. As a sophomore at RV College of Engineering, I have already demonstrated proficiency in Python and hands-on experience with LLM-based tools like Ollama and Anthropic API through projects such as Meridian and Anchor.

In my role at KuKClean, I set up 20+ product listings end-to-end on the e-commerce storefront and built Figma wireframes for new product pages. Additionally, I developed Meridian, a multi-agent orchestration system that produced cited due-diligence memos using Ollama (qwen2.5:7b). This project not only showcased my proficiency in Python but also highlighted my experience with prompt engineering for structured outputs and LLM-based tool integration.

At Lucenta AI, I am eager to contribute by building internal prototypes that automate research tasks using LLMs and iterating on prompts to ensure high-quality output. My background and ongoing education make me confident in delivering value as an intern in your team. Thank you for considering my application. I look forward to the opportunity to discuss how my skills can benefit Lucenta AI.
